question: Is the NuScale 60-year design life claim from Ch.01 adequately supported by the Ch.05 RPV integrity analysis?

nuscale_ch05_0013.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0013.summary: This section lists references and standards used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report regarding the integrity of the reactor coolant boundary. It includes guidelines and reports from the Electric Power Research Institute and the American Society of Mechanical Engineers, as well as tables summarizing relevant ASME code cases for materials and components used in nuclear facility applications.

nuscale_ch01_0050.title: Diesel Generator Buildings
nuscale_ch01_0050.summary: The Diesel Generator Buildings (DGBs) in the NuScale Power Plant each house a single backup diesel generator and their auxiliary equipment, serving as support structures without any safety-related systems or functions supporting Engineered Safety Features (ESFs). The DGBs contain diesel engines, generators, HVAC systems, and maintenance areas.

nuscale_ch05_0016.title: Pressure-Temperature Limits, Pressurized Thermal Shock, and Charpy Upper-Shelf Energy Data and Analyses
nuscale_ch05_0016.summary: This section outlines the basis for establishing operational pressure and temperature limits for the reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) to comply with regulatory requirements over the plant’s 60-year lifespan. It describes the methodology used to calculate pressure-temperature limit curves, accounting for vessel embrittlement, and provides the relevant figures and tables that define acceptable operating conditions to prevent non-ductile failure of the reactor vessel.

nuscale_ch05_0017.title: Reactor Vessel Integrity
nuscale_ch05_0017.summary: This section outlines the procedures and regulatory compliance measures to ensure the integrity of the NuScale reactor vessel throughout its 60-year design life. It details the development of operating procedures to maintain pressure-temperature limits, evaluates the vessel’s resistance to pressurized thermal shock and upper-shelf energy degradation, and confirms that all predicted values remain within regulatory safety criteria.

nuscale_ch01_0049.title: Central Utility Building
nuscale_ch01_0049.summary: The Central Utility Building (CUB) is a non-safety-related structure that houses essential utility services for the plant, such as chiller equipment, air systems, demineralized water equipment, and security functions. The section also describes the Diesel Generator Buildings (DGBs), which provide housing and support for backup diesel generators but do not contain safety-related systems. Additionally, the plant design incorporates human factors engineering to minimize human error and optimize control room operations through automation and digital technologies.

nuscale_ch01_0419.title: DSRS 10.2.3, Rev 0: Turbine Rotor IntegrityII.2 Fracture Toughness
nuscale_ch01_0419.summary: This section explains that the NuScale plant design uses physical barriers to protect safety-related structures, systems, and components (SSCs) from turbine missile effects, as specified in regulatory guidance. As a result, requirements related to turbine rotor integrity, including fracture toughness and associated inspections, are not applicable to the NuScale design.

nuscale_ch01_0073.title: Departures
nuscale_ch01_0073.summary: This section outlines the interface requirements between the NuScale Power Plant certified design and the site-specific design provided in the combined license (COL) application. It describes the types of interface requirements (Conceptual Design Information and COL information items), lists the necessary information for licensing and operation not included in the certified design, and specifies that COL applicants must provide a list of any departures from the certified design.

nuscale_ch05_0015.title: Reactor Vessel Materials
nuscale_ch05_0015.summary: The Reactor Vessel section describes the NuScale Power Module’s reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which houses the reactor core, steam generators, and pressurizer within a containment vessel. The RPV is a critical barrier to fission product release and is constructed from materials specified by the ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code to ensure integrity and safety throughout the plant’s design life. Material specifications for the RPV and its components are detailed in referenced tables and comply with stringent industry standards.

nuscale_ch01_0430.title: SRP 10.3.6, Rev 3: Steam and Feedwater System MaterialsII.2 Fracture Toughness of Class 2 and 3 Components
nuscale_ch01_0430.summary: This section states that the NuScale design does not contain any Class 2 or 3 components, so the fracture toughness requirements of SRP 10.3.6, Rev 3 are not applicable. The document also summarizes NuScale’s conformance or departures from various regulatory criteria for steam and feedwater system materials, noting specific applicability and compliance for each criterion.

nuscale_ch01_0120.title: 1.8.2 Departures
nuscale_ch01_0120.summary: Section 1.8.2 describes the requirement for a Combined License (COL) applicant referencing the NuScale Power Plant design certification to provide a list of any departures from the certified design. This ensures that any differences between the certified design and the site-specific implementation are clearly identified and reviewed as part of the licensing process.

nuscale_ch05_0018.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0018.summary: This section outlines the inspection, shipment, installation, operating conditions, inservice surveillance, and threaded fastener requirements for the NuScale reactor vessel, referencing relevant ASME and ASTM standards. It specifies procedures for maintaining vessel integrity during transport and operation, and lists the key codes and standards used for compliance.

nuscale_ch05_0007.title: Integrity of Reactor Coolant Boundary
nuscale_ch05_0007.summary: This section describes the design features of each NuScale Power Module (NPM) that ensure the integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) throughout the plant’s design life. It details the components and systems included in the RCPB, such as the reactor pressure vessel, containment isolation valves, safety valves, and associated piping, all designed to meet regulatory definitions and maintain pressure containment.

nuscale_ch01_0123.title: Table 1.9-2: Conformance with Regulatory Guides (Continued)
nuscale_ch01_0123.summary: This section of Table 1.9-2 summarizes NuScale’s conformance with various NRC Regulatory Guides related to quality assurance, seismic design, instrumentation, and power systems. It specifies whether NuScale’s design fully, partially, or does not conform to each guide, provides justifications or references to applicable standards, and notes relevant FSAR sections for further details. Some guides are marked not applicable due to differences in standards or design approaches.

nuscale_ch05_0025.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0025.summary: This section details the materials used in the NuScale pressurizer, including low alloy steel for the baffle plate, stainless steel and nickel-based alloys for cladding and components, all selected for corrosion resistance and compatibility with operating environments. It also references relevant material specifications from the ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code (BPVC) and cites a nuclear energy source as a reference.

nuscale_ch01_0172.title: DSRS 6.6, Rev 0: Inservice Inspection and Testing of Class 2 and 3 ComponentsII.1 Components Subject to Inspection
nuscale_ch01_0172.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory requirements for inservice inspection and testing of Class 2 and 3 components. It confirms that the NuScale design meets all relevant criteria in DSRS 6.6, including inspection scope, accessibility, examination methods, intervals, evaluation, pressure tests, and structural supports, while noting that certain SRP sections are not applicable due to differences in containment design.

nuscale_ch05_0008.title: Compliance with Codes and Code Cases
nuscale_ch05_0008.summary: This section describes how the NuScale Power Module (NPM) and its reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) components comply with relevant U.S. regulations, specifically 10 CFR 50.55a and associated General Design Criteria. It confirms that these components are designed, fabricated, and tested to ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code (BPVC) Section III, 2013 Edition standards, with appropriate quality classifications and adherence to NRC guidance.

nuscale_ch01_0383.title: SRP 9.4.5, Rev 3: Engineered Safety Feature Ventilation SystemAll Various
nuscale_ch01_0383.summary: This section explains that the NuScale design does not use Engineered Safety Feature (ESF) ventilation systems for mitigating design basis accidents, instead relying on nonsafety-related normal ventilation systems that meet regulatory guidelines but are not credited for offsite dose limits. It also notes that fire protection program requirements are either not applicable or are the responsibility of future license applicants, with partial conformance only where NuScale uses current guidance documents.

nuscale_ch05_0011.title: Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary Inservice Inspection and Testing
nuscale_ch05_0011.summary: Threaded fasteners in key reactor coolant system components use Alloy 718, chosen for its compatibility with borated water and resistance to stress corrosion cracking, in accordance with ASME BPVC standards. Preservice and inservice inspection and testing of pressure-retaining components follow ASME BPVC Section XI and relevant regulatory requirements, with inspection intervals and code editions specified in the inspection program to ensure ongoing integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary.

nuscale_ch01_0385.title: SRP 9.5.1.1, Rev 0: Fire Protection ProgramII.2 Fire Protection Program Considerations for License Renewal (Including Appendix B)
nuscale_ch01_0385.summary: This section explains that the fire protection program considerations for license renewal (SRP 9.5.1.1, II.2) are not applicable to the NuScale design, as they only apply to reactor licensees seeking license renewal. The NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report clarifies that these criteria do not pertain to their application.

nuscale_ch01_0175.title: DSRS 6.6, Rev 0: Inservice Inspection and Testing of Class 2 and 3 ComponentsII.4 Inspection Intervals
nuscale_ch01_0175.summary: The section confirms that the NuScale reactor design fully conforms to the requirements for inservice inspection and testing of Class 2 and 3 components, including inspection intervals, as outlined in DSRS 6.6, Rev 0. It notes that certain SRP sections related to ice condensers and suppression pools are not applicable to NuScale, as its design does not use these features.

edges.description: Section A lists references, including ASME and EPRI guidelines, that are cited to support the technical analyses and code compliance described in Section B regarding RPV integrity and weld requirements.

Section A lists references, including industry guidelines and standards, that underpin the technical basis for the RPV integrity analysis described in Section B, which in turn provides procedural and compliance details relevant to supporting the 60-year design life claim.

Section B describes the development of operating procedures to ensure compliance with the pressure-temperature limits analyzed in Section A, thereby supporting the integrity of the reactor vessel over its design life.

Section B provides references to industry standards and guidelines that are used as technical bases in the RPV integrity analysis, which could be used to support the 60-year design life claim in Section A if cited or relied upon.

Section B provides detailed analyses and code compliance information regarding the reactor pressure vessel's integrity, which is necessary to support the 60-year design life claim referenced in Section A.

Section A outlines the interface requirements and boundaries for certified design components, while Section B lists references used in the RPV integrity analysis, with both sections connected through their reliance on certified design definitions and requirements to ensure the integrity and longevity of the reactor pressure vessel as claimed in the design life.

Section B provides detailed analyses and code compliance information regarding the reactor pressure vessel's integrity, which supports the general 60-year design life claim made in Section A by demonstrating that the RPV meets relevant structural and inspection requirements.

Section A describes the materials and structural components of the NuScale reactor pressure vessel, while Section B lists references to industry standards and guidelines (such as EPRI and ASME) that are likely used to justify or support the integrity analysis of those materials, thus connecting the design details to the regulatory and technical basis for long-term integrity claims.

Section A provides a general description of the reactor pressure vessel (RPV) and its role in the reactor system, while Section B specifies detailed requirements and analyses for RPV integrity, such as pressure-temperature limits and weld examinations, which are necessary to support long-term design life claims.

Section B provides references to industry standards and guidelines that underpin the technical basis for the RPV integrity analysis discussed in Section A, thereby supporting the adequacy of the design life claim.

Section B provides detailed analyses and data on RPV integrity (including pressure-temperature limits and fracture toughness) that serve as technical evidence to support the 60-year design life claim made in Section A.

Section A outlines the interface requirements and identifies the certified design components, while Section B lists references that provide technical standards and guidelines relevant to the integrity analysis of those components, connecting the design life claim to the supporting technical basis.

Section A discusses the interface requirements between the certified design and site-specific design, referencing Section 1.2, which is elaborated in Section B by describing the general plant structures such as the Diesel Generator Buildings and Central Utility Building included in the certified design.

Section A outlines the interface requirements and identifies the certified design scope, which includes the reactor pressure vessel (RPV), while Section B provides detailed RPV integrity analyses (e.g., weld requirements, ASME code compliance) that support the design's claimed durability and safety, thus connecting the general design life claim to its technical substantiation.

Section A discusses regulatory conformance for turbine rotor integrity and its inapplicability to certain safety-related SSCs, while Section B outlines the interface requirements between the certified design and site-specific design, with both sections referencing the scope and boundaries of SSCs included in the certified design.

Section B provides the technical description and context for the reactor pressure vessel (RPV) materials and integrity, which are necessary to evaluate whether the 60-year design life claim in Section A is adequately supported by the RPV integrity analysis.

Section B defines the interface boundaries and certified design scope, which is a prerequisite for understanding whether the RPV integrity analysis in Section A is relevant to supporting the 60-year design life claim in Ch.01.

Section A identifies the certified design boundaries and components, including the reactor pressure vessel (RPV), while Section B provides detailed information about the RPV's materials and integrity, thus Section B specifies technical details for the general design claims in Section A.

Section A describes the materials and structural components of the NuScale reactor pressure vessel (RPV), while Section B documents the regulatory conformance status for material fracture toughness requirements, explicitly stating that certain criteria are not applicable due to the absence of Class 2 or 3 components in the NuScale design.
Section A provides detailed information about the reactor pressure vessel (RPV) materials and configuration, which are part of the certified design boundaries referenced in Section B.

Section B identifies the boundaries and components included in the certified design, which is necessary context for Section A's statements about the applicability of regulatory criteria (such as the absence of Class 2 or 3 components) in the NuScale design.

Section A cites ASME NQA-1-2008/1a-2009 Addenda as a quality assurance requirement for RPV shipment and installation, which is also referenced in Section B as a standard for nuclear facility applications, indicating both sections rely on the same regulatory standard for quality assurance.

Section A describes inspection, shipment, and storage requirements for the RPV referencing ASME codes, while Section B details specific pressure-temperature and weld examination requirements per ASME BPVC, with both sections connecting through their reliance on ASME code compliance for RPV integrity over the design life.

Section A describes the design features intended to maintain reactor coolant pressure boundary integrity for the plant's design life, while Section B lists technical references (such as EPRI and ASME standards) that underpin and support the analysis and claims made in Section A.
Section B provides detailed analyses and compliance information regarding reactor vessel integrity and welds, which supports the general design life integrity claim made in Section A.

Section A lists regulatory guides and quality assurance criteria that the design claims to conform with, while Section B provides references to technical standards and guidelines (such as ASME NQA-1 and EPRI documents) that are cited as part of the RPV integrity analysis supporting those conformance claims.
Section B provides technical analysis and compliance details regarding RPV integrity, which serves as supporting evidence for the 60-year design life claim made in Section A.

Section B asserts that the design features maintain reactor coolant pressure boundary integrity for the plant's design life, while Section A describes inspection and handling requirements that help ensure this integrity is preserved, thus supporting the 60-year design life claim.

Section A describes the RPV's compliance with ASME and NQA-1 quality assurance and handling requirements, which directly supports Section B's claim of conformance with Regulatory Guide 1.28 on Quality Assurance Program Criteria for design and construction.

Section A describes the design features ensuring reactor coolant pressure boundary integrity for the plant's design life, while Section B documents that the relevant quality assurance and regulatory conformance (including Section 5.2) are met, thus Section B supports the adequacy of the integrity analysis in Section A for the 60-year design life claim.

Section A describes the materials and components of the RPV and pressurizer, while Section B lists references (such as EPRI and ASME standards) that provide the technical basis for evaluating the integrity and reliability of those materials over the reactor's design life, thus connecting the design/materials (A) to the supporting technical standards and guidelines (B).

Section A describes the materials and components of the RPV and references where their specifications are detailed, while Section B provides analysis and code compliance information for RPV attachments and welds, thus Section B supports the adequacy of the RPV's integrity as claimed in Section A by providing evidence of compliance with ASME code requirements.

Section B provides references to industry standards and guidelines that are cited in Section A to demonstrate conformance with regulatory criteria for reactor pressure vessel integrity, which is relevant to supporting the 60-year design life claim.

Section B provides technical analysis and code compliance details for the reactor pressure vessel (RPV) integrity, which are necessary to support the 60-year design life claim referenced in Section A.

Section A asserts compliance with regulatory codes for RPV integrity and Section B lists the technical references (such as ASME and EPRI guidelines) that substantiate this compliance, directly supporting the adequacy of the design life claim.

Section A establishes that the RPV and associated components comply with ASME BPVC Section III as required by regulation, while Section B provides specific details on how certain RPV features and welds meet the relevant ASME BPVC requirements, thus supporting the overall compliance claim.

Section A describes the selection and qualification of RPV threaded fastener materials and explicitly references Section B, which lists the industry standards and guidelines (such as ASME and EPRI documents) that underpin the material choices and inspection requirements.

Section A describes the material selection and ASME code compliance for RPV threaded fasteners, while Section B details the inspection and code compliance for RPV stud welds and attachments, both providing evidence of adherence to ASME BPVC requirements that support the integrity analysis relevant to the 60-year design life claim.
Section B provides technical details and analysis of the reactor pressure vessel's integrity, which are necessary to support or challenge the 60-year design life claim made in Section A.

Section B provides references to industry standards and guidelines that are likely used as technical bases in the RPV integrity analysis discussed in Section A, thereby supporting the adequacy of the design life claim.

Section B provides technical details and analysis of the reactor pressure vessel (RPV) integrity, which are necessary to support and potentially satisfy the 60-year design life claim referenced in Section A.

Section A describes the materials and components of the RPV and pressurizer, while Section B states that these components comply with ASME BPVC and 10 CFR 50.55a requirements, thus Section B provides regulatory compliance evidence for the design details in Section A.

Section A describes the materials used in the RPV upper shell and head, while Section B specifies that the threaded fasteners in these components conform to ASME BPVC requirements and are compatible with the reactor environment, thus providing supporting evidence for the integrity and longevity of the RPV as claimed in the design life.

Section A provides specific material and design details of the RPV and pressurizer components, while Section B clarifies that certain SRP acceptance criteria related to ice condenser and pressure suppression pool systems are not applicable to the NuScale design, indicating that the referenced regulatory requirements in B do not constrain or directly support the design life claim discussed in A.

Section B provides evidence that the RPV and associated components comply with ASME Code and regulatory requirements, which supports the long-term integrity claims relevant to the 60-year design life stated in Section A.

Section A and Section B both clarify that certain SRP sections (ice condenser containment and ESF ventilation systems) are not applicable to the NuScale design, indicating that these regulatory requirements do not pertain to the RPV integrity analysis or the 60-year design life claim.

Section B provides detailed information on the materials and inspection/testing practices for RPV integrity, which supports the long-term design life claim made in Section A by addressing regulatory conformance and material suitability.

Section A lists the components subject to inspection, while Section B specifies the inspection intervals for those components, both within the context of inservice inspection requirements for Class 2 and 3 components.

Section B provides specific material and design details for RPV threaded fasteners that demonstrate compliance with the ASME BPVC requirements referenced in Section A, thereby supporting the overall integrity claims for the reactor coolant pressure boundary.

Section A demonstrates compliance with regulatory codes for RPV integrity, which is a prerequisite for substantiating the 60-year design life claim in Section B, but Section B is not directly relevant as it addresses inapplicable inspection criteria for other containment types.

Section B provides technical details on the materials and standards for RPV threaded fasteners, which are relevant to demonstrating the long-term integrity required for the 60-year design life claim referenced in Section A.

Section A and Section B both discuss the inapplicability of ESF ventilation systems for the NuScale design in the context of different regulatory criteria, but neither addresses the 60-year RPV design life or its integrity analysis, so they are related only by referencing the same design feature.

Section A provides detailed information on the materials and inspection requirements for RPV threaded fasteners, which are a component of the overall RPV integrity analysis that supports the 60-year design life claim in Section B.
